author: Besting
text: REVISED 2026-06-06: a fresh landing's substrate is SEEDED by copying the app-level default-config/ folder into balls/config (not hand-built defaults). The seed carries tracker/delivery/builtin plugin wiring, so they are ordinary landing entries — no runtime plugin defaults. (Seed mechanism itself = new sibling task; this task just creates the landing/store branches + binding and invokes the seed.) spec bl-2e26 §1/§12.

author: Aurally
text: Coordination (2026-06-06, after bl-35f7 landed c6b9569): the task-op DISPATCH that resolves the terminus + builds the §7 binding now lives in src/mutate.rs (bl-35f7, the §9 mutating-verb wiring) — it post-dates this ball's 'WHAT amends' list (bl-e755/bl-5c65/bl-be5f/bl-4450), so don't miss it. mutate::run currently does ALL of the following against the single clone.operating()/STATE_BRANCH and must be split to the two-branch model: (1) Registry::at(&operating).resolve(op,phase) — the plugin chain lives on the LANDING (config/plugins, §2), so resolve from the landing checkout; (2) the CHANGE worktree + Git::at(&operating).seal — task ops seal onto the STORE (tasks_branch), so the terminus is the store checkout; (3) it builds the binding via checkout::binding(operating, invocation, origin_of(operating), STATE_BRANCH). NOTE: bl-35f7 made checkout::binding + checkout::origin_of pub(crate) and mutate REUSES them — so the new {remote,tasks_branch,store,landing,invocation_path} binding has ONE construction point (checkout::binding); update its signature there and fix the two call sites (checkout prime/sync + mutate). So 'task ops seal onto the store' = editing mutate.rs, not just substrate/engine.

author: Kenning
text: Coordination (2026-06-06, after bl-154f landed 9e65ab8): the §13 diffless post facts couple to this ball's 'retire operating/ + lifecycle seal targets' work. Engine::diffless_inner (src/lifecycle.rs:233-236) reads self.terminus.head() BEFORE and AFTER pre to bracket previous_commit/commit for the sync/prime post wire. Two corrections fall out of this ball: (1) TERMINUS TARGET — those head reads must resolve the STORE branch (tasks_branch) HEAD, since §13 specs the facts as 'the store branch HEAD before and after the op'. run_chain currently builds terminus = Git::at(clone.operating()); when operating/ is retired, point the diffless terminus at the store checkout so the facts bracket the store, not the landing/config branch. (2) FRESH-CLONE NULL — §13: 'previous_commit ... null on a fresh-clone prime where nothing pre-existed.' Once the store branch can be unborn independently of the founded landing, head()-before-pre will FAIL on an unborn ref and abort the op (OpError::Terminus). It must degrade to None, and previous_commit must be representable as null: Sealed.previous_commit (lifecycle.rs:52) and SealFacts.previous_commit (wire.rs:84) are non-optional &str today; make them Option<&str> — the wire Payload.previous_commit (wire.rs:110) is ALREADY Option, so only the Sealed→SealFacts→wire path forcing Some(...) (wire.rs:163) needs to thread the None through. Today this is unreachable (single 'balls' branch; substrate::found commits once before the engine runs, so HEAD always exists), so bl-154f is correct as landed — these are the deltas that go live when the store ref can be unborn.
